title: feat(gsta-59): parallel multi-model orchestration, concurrency cap, and nth-fastest metrics
head: feat/gsta-59-parallel-orchestration
body:
Summary:
- Add Router(max_concurrency) to limit concurrent provider calls via an asyncio.Semaphore.
- Record nth-fastest latency metrics when returning the first N responses: `multi.nth_fastest_latency_ms` and `multi.nth_fastest_count`.
- Keep existing metrics: `multi.responses.returned`, `multi.tasks.cancelled`.
- Add unit test for nth-fastest metrics (tests/unit/test_router_metrics.py).

Why:
Enables true parallel multi-model orchestration with a tunable global concurrency cap and basic observability to measure how quickly the router returns the Nth successful response.

Testing:
- Added unit test to validate nth-fastest metrics.
- Performed local verification with small scripts (tools/) since `pytest` isn't installed in the run environment.

Next steps:
- Run CI to validate the rest of the suite.
- Consider richer metrics (histogram, per-provider tags) and a test helper to reset the metrics singleton between tests.
